content:
Hey I want to try and contribute to the kernel (I developed kernel modules before) and Im looking for any simple open issue that I can do to get my hands a bit dirty.
In the issues page in the github it seems that all the "good first issues" are taken (LOL) anyone got a simple issue that I can do to start getting a vibe?

There are at least a couple more lists of issues (Nova's and Tyr's) that you may want to take a look at as well:

  • https://docs.kernel.org/next/gpu/nova/core/todo.html
  • https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/issues/?label_name%5B%5D=tyr

I will add links in the webpage ("Contributing") to these and hopefully others in the future, in case it helps :)
Cc: @Danilo Krummrich @Daniel Almeida @Alice Ryhl et al.
IIRC recently @Andreas Hindborg mentioned he may have another one (now or in the future) too, so I can link to that in that case too.
Finally, there are other related projects I link at the top of https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing that have their own issue trackers too, e.g. https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/pin-init/issues
Cc: @Benno Lossin
Some of those projects are out-of-tree of course (like compilers), but e.g. pin-init sends the patches into the kernel, in case you want to work on something that ends up in the kernel tree.

And for our "good first issue" list, yeah, we add more from time to time as time allows. But you can think about similar issues you could tackle as a first patch by looking into old ones and going around the kernel Rust source code to see if you notice things that could be improved.

If you want to work on pin-init, there is this issue: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/pin-init/issues/85 that I want to implement for the next cycle. If you're experienced with rust proc macros, then that could be something you could work on. I already have a partially working solution in the dev/tuple-structs branch
if you're not too experienced with Rust, I'd not recommend working on pin-init, as it's quite complex. It also isn't interacting with the kernel, so you'd be writing just normal #![no_std] Rust code.

Finally, please note that there are other ways of contributing, not just adding new code! For instance, reviewing code and testing is something we will need more and more, and some people do like to do that, so I always mention it in case it is not obvious. It also gives credit in the Git log messages. The same applies to testing too.

Miguel Ojeda said:

There are at least a couple more lists of issues (Nova's and Tyr's) that you may want to take a look at as well:

  • https://docs.kernel.org/next/gpu/nova/core/todo.html
  • https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/issues/?label_name%5B%5D=tyr

I will add links in the webpage ("Contributing") to these and hopefully others in the future, in case it helps :)

@Alice Ryhl @Miguel Ojeda I'd like to work on tyr driver-related issues. I have an rk3588 device (nanopi R6C):

root@R6C:~# dmesg | grep -E "(model|mali|cpuinfo)"
[    5.249905] Machine model: FriendlyElec NanoPi R6C
[    7.375798] rockchip-cpuinfo cpuinfo: SoC        : 35880000
[    7.827015] mali fb000000.gpu: Kernel DDK version g25p0-00eac0
[    7.831435] mali fb000000.gpu: Register LUT 000a0800 initialized for GPU arch 0x000a0806
[    7.831468] mali fb000000.gpu: r0p0 status 5 not found in HW issues table;
[    7.831482] mali fb000000.gpu: falling back to closest match: r0p0 status 0
[    7.831495] mali fb000000.gpu: Execution proceeding normally with fallback match
[    7.831506] mali fb000000.gpu: GPU identified as 0x7 arch 10.8.6 r0p0 status 0
[    7.831811] mali fb000000.gpu: CSF_GPU_RESET_TIMEOUT is capped from 20666ms to 4500ms
[    7.831828] mali fb000000.gpu: KBASE_PRFCNT_ACTIVE_TIMEOUT is capped from 40000ms to 4500ms
[    7.831841] mali fb000000.gpu: KBASE_AS_INACTIVE_TIMEOUT is capped from 40000ms to 4500ms
[    7.831854] mali fb000000.gpu: CSF_FIRMWARE_STOP_TIMEOUT is capped from 40000ms to 4500ms
[    7.834597] mali fb000000.gpu: Probed as mali0
@Daniel Almeida
@Alvin Sun sorry, I’ve been OOO for a few days. Can you run glmark2 on our prototype and see what is crashing? That’s a good starting point to discover issues.
Please also try CTS while you’re at it
I think last time @Beata Michalska tried that, there were a lot of issues

@Daniel Almeida OK. I may need some time to set up the system environment. I'll update you
once I have progress.
I suggest TFTP+NFS
I will email you some instructions on setting this up

Daniel Almeida said:

I suggest TFTP+NFS

For U-Boot? I tried UEFI on it before, and grub for switching kernels and systems works
better for me. It's currently running OpenWrt as a router, so I need time to set up a replacement device.
Yes, Tftp for u-boot in order to load the kernel
If you have another setup, feel free to use that

Daniel Almeida said:

Alvin Sun sorry, I’ve been OOO for a few days. Can you run glmark2 on our prototype and see what is crashing? That’s a good starting point to discover issues.

I tried to boot an Armbian system with tyr, but the graphical service failed to start (with the same kernel, switching to panthor works).

# dmesg | grep tyr
[    1.096997] tyr fb000000.gpu: supply sram not found, using dummy regulator
[    1.303536] tyr fb000000.gpu: mali-unknown id 0xa867 major 0x67 minor 0x0 status 0x5
[    1.303555] tyr fb000000.gpu: Features: L2:0x7120306 Tiler:0x809 Mem:0x301 MMU:0x2830 AS:0xff
[    1.303562] tyr fb000000.gpu: shader_present=0x0000000000050005 l2_present=0x0000000000000001 tiler_present=0x0000000000000001
[    1.304031] tyr fb000000.gpu: Tyr initialized correctly.

I also have error messages related to the display service. I'll send them to
you privately. @Daniel Almeida

Awesome! Apparently it’s something to investigate and perhaps fix.

Can you

a) open an issue on our issue tracker
b) assign it to yourself
c) try something simpler, like a single frame of headless kmscube
d) instrument Tyr and PanVK to gather more information
I suggest instrumenting the code around the GROUP_SUBMIT ioctl both in panvk and in Tyr
Looking at your log more closely, is the display driver running?
Let’s also move that discussion to the issue tracker so that we don’t spam this thread
Sure. I’ll try that following your suggestion.
Wow didn't expect for so many responses and help @Miguel Ojeda where can I find code I can review?

Shachar said:

Wow didn't expect for so many responses and help Miguel Ojeda where can I find code I can review?

Here: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/
you might also find the following useful (perhaps after some time getting comfortable the LKML workflow): https://people.kernel.org/monsieuricon/lore-lei-part-1-getting-started

Daniel Almeida said:

I suggest TFTP+NFS

I will email you some instructions on setting this up

I would like to get that one, when you have some free time could you send them over work@onurozkan.dev?
@Onur Özkan yep, at fosdem atm, but will do so shortly (please ping again if I forget)
Hi, im currently looking for an open issue to work on. I have some good rust knowledge and experience and am looking to get more involved. I would appreciate any feedback.

Christopher Erleigh said:

Hi, im currently looking for an open issue to work on. I have some good rust knowledge and experience and am looking to get more involved. I would appreciate any feedback.

You can look for "good first issue" on the GH repo to get used to with LKML if you haven't send a patch before.

Mitchell Levy said:

you might also find the following useful (perhaps after some time getting comfortable the LKML workflow): https://people.kernel.org/monsieuricon/lore-lei-part-1-getting-started

I think b4 is the tool of choice for many nowadays: https://b4.docs.kernel.org/en/latest/

It is full-featured and quite powerful for working with LKML.

The idea of adding a Rust reference driver seems quite interesting to me as a device driver developer with some experience with some core kernel subsystems. Are there any opportunities to contribute in this area at the moment?
@Onur Özkan sent your instructions to setup the hardware. Please note that they are for the rock5b, but you can adapt that to other boards
has anyone thought about hwmon abstractions?

Daniel Almeida said:

Onur Özkan sent your instructions to setup the hardware. Please note that they are for the rock5b, but you can adapt that to other boards

Just read it now and still very useful, thanks a lot!

I think I can make the setup declarative on NixOS and write it up a doc for both the orange pi 5 (from my side) and the rock5b (from your side). Then we can add more boards later if needed. Could turn into a generic guideline that works for other people as well.

Here's my setup with OrangePi 5: https://gist.github.com/onur-ozkan/91b3dd31cb7694387a982e5862a7b3ec. If anyone is using this board now or plans to in the future, I spent my Sunday so you don't have to. :slight_smile:

Daniel Almeida said:

Alvin Sun sorry, I’ve been OOO for a few days. Can you run glmark2 on our prototype and see what is crashing? That’s a good starting point to discover issues.

I tried to boot an Armbian system with tyr, but the graphical service failed to start (with the same kernel, switching to panthor works).

# dmesg | grep tyr
[    1.096997] tyr fb000000.gpu: supply sram not found, using dummy regulator
[    1.303536] tyr fb000000.gpu: mali-unknown id 0xa867 major 0x67 minor 0x0 status 0x5
[    1.303555] tyr fb000000.gpu: Features: L2:0x7120306 Tiler:0x809 Mem:0x301 MMU:0x2830 AS:0xff

I also have error messages related to the display service. I'll send them to
you privately. Daniel Almeida

the "mali-unknown" part should be fixed with this patch: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260210183812.261142-1-work@onurozkan.dev/
I'm compiling your patch and will verify it on my Nanopi R6C(rk3588s). I'll send you feedback when there are results.

@Onur Özkan GPU model is now correctly identified:

root@nanopi-r6s:~# dmesg | grep tyr
[    1.894322] tyr fb000000.gpu: supply sram not found, using dummy regulator
[    1.894527] tyr fb000000.gpu: mali-g610 id 0xa867 major 0x0 minor 0x0 status 0x5
[    1.894534] tyr fb000000.gpu: Features: L2:0x7120306 Tiler:0x809 Mem:0x301 MMU:0x2830 AS:0xff
